title: Root app layout omits required html and body tags
reasoning: In the Next App Router, the root layout is the document shell and must render the required <html> and <body> tags. This RootLayout returns children directly, so the application can fail Next's build/runtime validation for missing root layout tags even if a nested locale layout exists. Because this is the top-level app layout and the project ships with a Next build script, it is a release-blocking hazard.
reproduction: Run `pnpm --dir frontend build`; Next should validate the root app layout and report missing required html/body tags.
recommendation: Update `frontend/src/app/layout.tsx` so RootLayout returns an `<html>` element containing a `<body>{children}</body>`. Keep locale-specific providers or attributes in the nested locale layout only if Next still has a valid document shell at the root.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are linked for this feature, and the project metadata does not list an existing test or typecheck command. A build smoke test would catch this structural App Router requirement.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a CI build smoke check for `pnpm --dir frontend build`, or at minimum a focused test/static check that the root app layout renders html and body tags.
minimumFixScope: Change `frontend/src/app/layout.tsx` to provide the required root document tags.
